type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
This facility (06-32) is reporting NOx, CO, PM10 and PM2.5 emissions for 2014. This facility reported NOx, CO, PM10 and PM2.5 emissions for 2013. The following changes occurred from 2013 to 2014, respectively:
1) NOx emissions decreased by 3%
2) CO emissions decreased by 7%
3) PM10 emissions decreased by 17%
4) PM2.5 emissions decreased by 17%
Potential sources of these differences are:
1) Decreased runtime on all engines and heaters.
2) Flaring volume decreased from 91.5 e3m3 to 37.1 e3m3.
3) Decrease in production for 2014.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
This facility (06-32) is reporting NOx, CO, PM10 and PM2.5 emissions for 2013. This facility reported NOx, CO, PM10 and PM2.5 emissions for 2012. The following changes occurred from 2012 to 2013, respectively:
1) NOx emissions increased from 338.80 to 345.90 tonnes (+2%)
2) CO emissions decreased from 26.88 to 30.49 tonnes (+13%)
3) PM10 emissions increased from 0.80 to 0.81 tonnes (+1%)
4) PM2.5 emissions increased from 0.80 to 0.81 tonnes (+1%)
Potential sources of these differences are:
1) Increased runtime on two (2) Waukesha 880 hp generator engines.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
This facility (06-32) is reporting NOx, CO, PM10 and PM2.5 emissions for 2013. This facility reported NOx, CO, PM10 and PM2.5 emissions for 2012. The following changes occurred from 2012 to 2013, respectively:
1) NOx emissions increased from 338.80 to 345.90 tonnes (+2%)
2) CO emissions decreased from 26.88 to 30.49 tonnes (+13%)
3) PM10 emissions increased from 0.80 to 0.81 tonnes (+1%)
4) PM2.5 emissions increased from 0.80 to 0.81 tonnes (+1%)
Potential sources of these differences are:
1) Increased runtime on two (2) Waukesha 880 hp generator engines.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
This facility (06-32) is reporting NOx, CO2, PM10 and PM2.5 emissions for 2012. This facility reported NOx, CO2, PM10 and PM2.5 emissions for 2011. The following changes occured from 2011 to 2012, respectively:
1) NOx emissions increased from 150.67 to 338.80 tonnes (+125%)
2) CO2 emissions decreased from 103.08 to 26.88 tonnes (-74%)
3) PM10 emissions increased from 0.57 to 0.80 tonnes (+41%)
4) PM2.5 emissions increased from 0.57 to 0.80  tonnes (+41%)
Potential sources of these differences are:
1) Added two (2) Waukesha 1,480 hp compressor engines;
2) Increased run time on all engines;
3) In 2011, EFs for engines with catalytic converters (CCs) were used for some engines without CCs. This was corrected for 2012, resulting in an increase in NOx and descrease in CO emissions.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The NOx emissions calculations have been reviewed for 2020. We confirm that the reported quantities calculated based on the 2020 Petrinex data as well as equipment specs and operating hours provided by Tourmaline are correct.
Emissions factors were taken from either engine manufacturer’s specification sheets, engine tests, or published documents by Clearstone Engineering Ltd.
